item.text: The worktree already has untracked `node_modules/` and `pnpm-lock.yaml`; I’ll leave those alone unless the verification command naturally uses installed dependencies. Next I’m reading the implementation and tests to match the existing API.

The current code already covers the broad path, but the edge behavior is thin: runtime number validation can be clearer, and tests don’t lock down empty/single inputs, duplicates, default touching behavior, or order independence. I’m going to make those cases explicit and keep the public API unchanged.

I’m editing the coalescer to validate finite numeric endpoints with index-aware messages, then expanding the Vitest suite around the requested edge cases and the current default of merging touching intervals.
Implementation is patched. I’m adding tests now, including two orderings of the same input so deterministic sorting is covered directly.
The test coverage is now expanded. I’m running the test suite and TypeScript check separately so any failure points cleanly at either behavior or types.

item.text: The rerun exposed an existing expectation: this coalescer treats adjacent integer ranges as “touching” by default, so `[-3,-2]` and `[-1,4]` are expected to coalesce. I’m aligning the merge predicate with that existing test while keeping `mergeTouching: false` strict.

item.text:
Implemented the interval coalescer fixes and expanded edge-case coverage for empty/single inputs, negative and reversed ranges, duplicates, touching behavior, deterministic order, and invalid values. Added a local Vitest config so tests run inside the workspace instead of picking up the parent config.

Verification ran and passed:

- `pnpm test` - 13 tests passed
- `pnpm build` - TypeScript check passed
